_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 GA state legislators (priority batch). Every TRUE/FALSE cites a source URL; null where no cited evidence; never inferred from party. CANDIDACY-CURRENCY CATCH: Nabilah Islam Parkes resigned SD-7 on 2026-03-13 to run for Lieutenant Governor (status updated). Cross-party deviations scored as documented credit/contrary: Goodman NO on IVF (pro-life embryo, TRUE); Islam Parkes YES on ESG restrictions (TRUE); Gunter YES on IVF (FALSE) and NO on 2023 vouchers. Individual GA roll calls that were JS-blocked to automated fetch are nulled, not guessed.

profile.background: Newnan businessman (partner, Lott Metal & Commercial Roofing); U.S. Navy veteran; UGA marketing '02; former chief of staff to U.S. Rep. Lynn Westmoreland. GA State Senator since 2017 (SD-28, renumbered SD-6 in 2021-22 redistricting); Chairman of Senate Rules. Deacon at Newnan Presbyterian. Reelected 2024; running again in the May 19, 2026 GOP primary.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored 2026-06 from the Georgia Legislative Freedom Index 2024 (67%), SB202/SB319/SB140/HB1105/HB1018 sponsorship and roll calls, and his HB481 (LIFE Act) vote. Some individual roll calls (SB233 vouchers, SB63 bail) were not retrievable to a URL and left null. No cross-party deviation found.

matt-brass@GA.notes_append: 2026-06 evidence pass: voted YES HB481 (2019 LIFE Act/heartbeat, fetal personhood); co-sponsor SB202 (2021 Election Integrity Act), SB319 (2022 constitutional carry), SB140 (2023 minors gender-transition ban); voted YES HB1018 (2024 2nd Amendment Protection Act), HB1105 (2024 anti-sanctuary). Freedom Index 2024 = 67%. Not on GA Life Alliance 2026 cert list (absence, not a negative). No cross-party deviation found.

0.note: Voted YES on HB481 (2019 LIFE Act/heartbeat bill, which recognizes fetal personhood); campaign issues page affirms 'sanctity of life' and 'working to end abortion.'

2.note: Co-sponsor of SB140 (2023) banning gender-transition procedures for minors.

1.note: Co-sponsor of SB202 (2021 Election Integrity Act), whose signature provision tightened photo voter ID for absentee ballots.

3.note: SB202 (2021) restricted drop boxes and mail-in ballot access; Brass was a co-sponsor.

4.note: SB202 (2021) banned private (Zuckerbucks) election funding; Brass was a co-sponsor.

0.note: Voted YES on HB1018 (2024) Second Amendment Protection Act, a state measure resisting federal firearm regulation.

2.note: HB1018 (2024) resists federal overreach on firearms at the state level.
Voted YES on HB1105 (2024) Criminal Alien Track and Report Act (anti-sanctuary, ICE cooperation).

1.note: Voted YES on HB1018 (2024), which bars firearm-retailer merchant codes and gun registries.

profile.background: Blue Ridge banker (40-year career, executive at United Community Bank); deacon at Helping Hand Fellowship Church; 4th-generation North Georgian. GA State Rep HD-7 (Fannin/Gilmer/part Dawson) since Feb 2023 (won the special runoff over Sheree Ralston). Reelected 2024; first-term member with a thin but uniformly conservative roll-call record.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored 2026-06 from the Turning Point Action scorecard (94.57 lifetime), his 2025 RFRA/Riley Gaines/school-choice votes, and direct campaign statements. As a member seated only in Feb 2023, he was not present for SB202/SB319/SB140 (nulled, not guessed). 2024 immigration/bail/gun roll calls not retrievable to a URL and left null.

johnny-chastain@GA.notes_append: 2026-06 evidence pass (first-term member, thin record): voted YES SB36 (2025 GA RFRA), SB1/HB267 (2025 Riley Gaines Act, trans-athlete ban — zero R defectors), SB1/HB268/SB212 (2025 educational-freedom package). Campaign site states pro-life and church deacon. TPAction 94.57 lifetime. No cross-party deviation found. Pre-tenure marquee bills (SB202 2021, SB319 2022) nulled — not in office.

0.note: Campaign site, own words: 'pro-life and will work to protect the innocent & unborn.'

2.note: Voted YES on the 2025 Riley Gaines Act (HB267/SB1) banning transgender athletes from opposite-sex school sports; affirms biological sex.

0.note: Voted YES on the 2025 educational-freedom package (SB1, HB268, SB212) supporting school choice (per TPAction Educational Freedom subset).

1.note: Voted YES on SB36 (2025) Georgia Religious Freedom Restoration Act, providing conscience protections for religious belief.

set.office: State Senator SD-7 (resigned 2026-03-13; 2026 Democratic candidate for Lieutenant Governor)

profile.background: Atlanta-born daughter of Bangladeshi immigrants; GSU marketing BBA; political organizer (DNC, Clinton 2016; 2020 US House candidate). First Muslim woman, first South Asian woman, and youngest woman elected to the GA Senate (SD-7, Gwinnett, 2022). RESIGNED SD-7 on 2026-03-13 to run in the 2026 Democratic primary for Lieutenant Governor.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored 2026-06. CANDIDACY-CURRENCY UPDATE: she left the Senate on 2026-03-13 to run for Lieutenant Governor (status set running_higher_office). Documented progressive positions recorded as FALSE on matching conservative-rubric cells; one genuine cross-party deviation (voted FOR ESG investment restrictions) recorded as TRUE credit. Bills predating her 2023 tenure (SB202, SB319) were nulled — she could not have voted. iVoterGuide 'one man/one woman' language was a non-response prompt, NOT her statement, and was excluded.

nabilah-islam-parkes@GA.notes_append: 2026-06 evidence pass: resigned SD-7 on 2026-03-13 to run for Lt. Governor. Backed the Reproductive Freedom Act to repeal HB481 six-week ban; celebrated the ban being struck (own press release); EMILY's List Rising Star nominee. Voted AGAINST SB233 vouchers, AGAINST SB63 cash-bail expansion, AGAINST HB1105 anti-sanctuary; backs in-state tuition for undocumented residents; demanded a 2023 firearm-safety special session. DEVIATION (documented credit): voted FOR HB481 ESG investment restrictions. ACU-GA 14%.

0.note: Backed the Reproductive Freedom Act to repeal Georgia's six-week ban (HB481) and publicly celebrated the ban being struck down — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

4.note: EMILY's List Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star nominee — accepted backing/affiliation with a pro-abortion electoral organization.

0.note: Voted AGAINST SB233 (2024 ESA vouchers) and against HB1122 charter funding — opposes school-choice expansion.

4.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted FOR HB481 ESG investment restrictions, limiting ESG capture of state investments — documented credit despite party.

1.note: Voted AGAINST SB63 (2024) expanding cash-bail requirements / restricting bail funds — favors looser bail, contrary to the rubric position.

2.note: Voted AGAINST HB1105 (2024 anti-sanctuary detainer compliance) and backs in-state tuition for undocumented residents — opposes sanctuary-restriction position.

1.note: One of four Democrats demanding a 2023 special session on firearm-safety measures; supports gun-control restrictions, contrary to opposing red-flag/registration.

profile.background: Cogdell blueberry/agribusiness owner (Cogdell Berry Farm); UGA Agricultural Economics; deacon, First Baptist Church of Homerville. GA State Senator SD-8 (South Georgia) since 2021; Chairman of Senate Agriculture & Consumer Affairs. Running again in the May 19, 2026 GOP primary (challenger Ralph Tyre).
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored 2026-06 from the Georgia Legislative Freedom Index (83% 2024), LegiScan/Open States roll calls, and his campaign issues page. DEVIATION: voted NO on HB428 (2025 right-to-IVF) against a near-unanimous bill — a pro-life embryo stance (credit). Some individual roll calls (SB233 vouchers, SB63 bail) not retrievable to a URL and left null.

russ-goodman@GA.notes_append: 2026-06 evidence pass: campaign affirms life 'from conception to natural death.' Voted NO on HB428 (2025 right-to-IVF) — rightward deviation against a 53-1 bill (pro-life embryo concern). Voted YES SB140 (2023 minors gender ban), SB202 (2021 Election Integrity Act), HB1018 (2024 2A Protection Act), HB1105 (2024 anti-sanctuary); co-sponsor SB319 (2022 constitutional carry). Freedom Index 2024 = 83%. No liberal-side deviation found.

0.note: Campaign issues page, own words: 'stand firm to protect human life – from conception to natural death.'

2.note: Voted NO on HB428 (2025) codifying a right to IVF (against a 53-1 bill) — consistent with opposing IVF embryo discard practices.

3.note: Campaign statement protecting life 'to natural death' — opposes euthanasia/assisted suicide.

2.note: Voted YES on SB140 (2023) banning gender-transition procedures for minors.

1.note: Voted YES on SB202 (2021 Election Integrity Act), whose signature provision tightened photo voter ID for absentee ballots.

3.note: SB202 (2021) restricted drop boxes and mail-in access.

0.note: Voted YES on HB1018 (2024) Second Amendment Protection Act, resisting federal firearm regulation at the state level.

2.note: HB1018 (2024) resists federal overreach on firearms.
Voted YES on HB1105 (2024) Criminal Alien Track and Report Act (anti-sanctuary).

1.note: Voted YES on HB1018 (2024), barring firearm-retailer merchant codes and gun registries.

profile.background: Former Superior Court Judge and District Attorney of the Enotah Judicial Circuit (NE Georgia mountains); North Georgia College (chemistry), Woodrow Wilson College of Law (JD). GA State Rep HD-8 (Union/Towns/part White) since 2021; Chairman of the House Judiciary Committee. Reelected 2024; running again in the May 19, 2026 GOP primary.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored 2026-06 from the Georgia Legislative Freedom Index per-legislator record, TPAction (93.48 lifetime), and roll calls. NUANCES: voted YES on HB428 (2025 right-to-IVF, 172-0) recorded as FALSE on the IVF-opposition cell; voted NO on the 2023 SB233 voucher version (a documented deviation) while later backing 2025 educational-freedom bills — so the school-choice credit cell is left null. Some 2024 roll calls (SB202, SB63, HB1105, HB1018) not retrievable to a URL and left null.

stan-gunter@GA.notes_append: 2026-06 evidence pass: Judiciary Chair, former judge/DA. Voted YES SB140 (2023 minors gender ban), HB1084 (2022 divisive-concepts/CRT + trans-athlete), HB1178 (2022 Parents' Bill of Rights), SB36 (2025 GA RFRA), HB1053 (2024 anti-CBDC), HR780 (2024 citizen-voting), SB319 (2022 constitutional carry). DEVIATION: voted NO on the 2023 SB233 voucher version (later YES SB1 2025 ed-freedom). Voted YES HB428 (2025 right-to-IVF, 172-0) — recorded as contrary to the IVF-opposition cell. TPAction 93.48 lifetime.

2.note: Voted YES on HB428 (2025) codifying a right to IVF (House 172-0) — contrary to opposing IVF embryo discard, scored honestly despite the unanimous tally.
Voted YES on SB140 (2023, minors gender-transition ban) and HB1084 (2022), affirming biological sex.

1.note: Voted YES on HB1178 (2022) Parents' Bill of Rights (parental notification/consent).

2.note: Voted YES on HB1084 (2022) restricting 'divisive concepts' (CRT-style) instruction in K-12.

1.note: Voted YES on SB36 (2025) Georgia Religious Freedom Restoration Act (conscience protections).

0.note: Voted YES on HB1053 (2024) opposing a Central Bank Digital Currency (anti-CBDC).

1.note: Voted YES on HR780 (2024) reinforcing citizen-only voting / citizenship verification.

0.note: Voted YES on SB319 (2022) constitutional/permitless carry.
